tables.description: This sample attributes data table reports each sample's cell line ID, analyte type, molecular analyte, cell type, differentiation round if applicable, doxycycline status at collection, doxycycline start date (weeks) relative to experiment start, doxycycline end date (weeks) relative to experiment start, and collection timepoint relative to experiment start (weeks).

variables.name: COLLECTION_TIME
variables.description: Collection timepoint relative to experiment start (in weeks). For neural diff (ND) relative to differentiation start.

variables.name: DIFF_ROUND
variables.description: Differentiation Round (ND) or "none"  for undifferentiated iPSC

variables.name: DOX_END
variables.description: Dox end relative to experiment start (in weeks) or N/A for nodox. For neural diff (ND) relative to differentiation start.

variables.name: DOX_START
variables.description: Dox start relative to experiment start (in weeks) or N/A for nodox. For neural diff (ND) relative to differentiation start.

variables.name: DOX_STATUS
variables.description: Dox treatment ongoing at time of collection?

tables.description: This subject consent data table contains subject IDs, consent information, biological sex, and subject aliases to map subjects to public repositories.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: The Subject ID used in the source repository

variables.name: SUBJECT_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where the subject originates

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
